title: Re-fetch memory views after the heap can grow
description: Treat any JS TypedArray view of WASM memory as invalidated whenever the module may call memory.grow.
body: Use when JS reads WASM linear memory through a TypedArray (e.g. new Uint8Array(memory.buffer)). Do not cache that view across a call that can allocate, because memory.grow replaces the underlying ArrayBuffer and detaches the old one, so the cached view reads stale or throws on a detached buffer. Actions: re-create the view from memory.buffer after any WASM call that may grow the heap, or copy data out immediately; keep views short-lived. Validation signals include a test that grows memory between obtaining and using a view and still reads correct bytes. Risk boundary: reading through a detached buffer yields wrong data or exceptions intermittently; refresh views after potential growth and verify under a grow-triggering workload.
